title: An Ordinary Person's PhD Application Journey: Prologue
summary: At a quiet moment in front of the computer, someone uncertain about the road ahead begins to reflect and document the start of the journey.

sections.heading: Why I Decided to Write This
paragraphs: This post is the beginning of a longer record of my PhD application journey. I do not want to present it as a dramatic story or a polished success narrative. Instead, I want to document the uncertainty, the small decisions, and the real thoughts that appear before any clear outcome exists.
For me, writing is a way to slow down, organize the noise in my mind, and leave an honest trace of where this journey actually began.

sections.heading: Where I Am Right Now
paragraphs: At this stage, I am still standing at the entrance. I have questions about research direction, academic ability, future location, and whether I am truly prepared for the long path ahead.
What feels most real right now is not certainty, but a mixture of curiosity, hesitation, pressure, and hope.

sections.heading: What I Hope to Figure Out
points: Whether pursuing a PhD is the right path for me rather than simply a socially acceptable choice.
What kind of research problems I genuinely want to spend several years working on.
How to prepare in a practical way, including research output, applications, and communication with potential advisors.
How to stay emotionally steady while facing comparison, rejection, and uncertainty.

sections.heading: What This Series Will Record
points: My changing thoughts about research and academia.
Preparation notes, decisions, and application milestones.
Moments of self-doubt, adjustment, and small progress.
Lessons that may be useful for other students on a similar path.

sections.heading: A Small Promise to Myself
paragraphs: I hope this series remains honest. If the journey becomes difficult, I want to write about the difficulty. If I make progress, I want to describe the process instead of only the result.
This prologue is not a conclusion. It is only a quiet starting point.
